title: AI trade making room for rest of market as S&P 500 equal-weigh index climbs
excerpt: A sell-off in chipmakers has created opportunities elsewhere and offered a hint at the AI trade's future.
content_html:
Spread the wealth: The AI trade's biggest winners are making room for the rest of the market

By Joe Ciolli

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Author of the First Trade newsletter

Shhh… be quiet. The stock market is trying to tell us something.

The message starts with the chipmaker space, which saw an electrifying more-than-100% surge in 2026 before hitting a wall in recent weeks. That sell-off has coincided with a rebound for hyperscalers, which have forged a market rivalry of sorts with chipmakers.

But there have also been encouraging signs of life outside the AI trade. Since the start of June, the equal-weighted S&P 500 has been handily outperforming its market-cap-weighted counterpart. The takeaway? Stock strength has spread beyond a few winners to a broader portion of the market.

It's a marked departure from recent history, which saw immense cap-weighted S&P 500 outperformance since the AI boom took hold in 2023:

The message is clear: investors are broadening their bets. The AI trade is entering a new phase, and the rally is spreading beyond its traditional leaders. If history is any indication, the broadening is a bullish sign. It implies that confidence is spreading across the market, and it naturally hedges rallies, making them more durable.

Morgan Stanley CIO Mike Wilson has, in typical fashion, been all over this. He's been beating the drum on a market broadening for weeks. A recent report detailed four reasons why:

  1. Accelerating earnings growth across more sectors
  2. Expanding AI adoption
  3. An eventual decline in oil prices, and
  4. A Fed the firm expects to remain on hold with rate hikes

Wilson takes it a step further and pinpoints consumer discretionary and transports as his two top sector picks, noting that they've already beaten the S&P 500 by 12% over the past two months.

What's more, Wilson thinks the AI broadening has room for a hyperscaler comeback. He notes that hyperscalers already ripped the Band-Aid off by pledging immense capex for the years ahead, while chipmakers are only just now feeling the pinch.

It's a sentiment that Ben Snider, the US stock-strategy chief at Goldman Sachs, recently shared with BI. His argument was more valuation-focused, pointing out that hyperscalers were recently trading near the very bottom of its long-term range, and due for a bounce back.

For investors willing to listen, the market's message isn't that the AI trade is fading. It's that leadership is changing in a healthy way. The best rallies don't rely on a single group of stocks to carry them. They find new leaders, draw in new sectors, and broaden their base. The current one looks to be doing just that.

Read next

Joe Ciolli

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Joe is an executive editor at Business Insider and the author of First Trade, a daily markets newsletter. Sign up here.He oversees the newsroom's markets and investing coverage, and previously ran the economy and finance teams. He started at Business Insider as a reporter in April 2017.Before joining BI, he was a stocks reporter at Bloomberg, where he also worked on teams focusing on foreign exchange, bonds and M&A. Before Bloomberg, he worked as an investment banking analyst at CIBC World Markets and Navigant Capital Advisors.Joe holds an MA in journalism from Stanford University and a BSBA from Washington University in St. Louis.

sentences.zh: 摩根士丹利

content_html_zh:
传播财富：人工智能行业的最大赢家正在为市场的其他部分腾出空间

作者：Joe Ciolli 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 第一贸易通讯作者

嘘…… 安静。 股市正试图告诉我们一些事情。

这一信息首先从芯片制造商领域开始，该领域在 2026 年实现了超过 100% 的惊人增长，但在最近几周陷入困境。 这次抛售恰逢超大规模企业的反弹，这些企业与芯片制造商形成了某种市场竞争。

但人工智能行业之外也出现了令人鼓舞的迹象。 自 6 月初以来，等权重标准普尔 500 指数的表现一直轻松优于市值加权指数。 外卖？ 股票的强势已经从少数赢家扩展到更广泛的市场部分。

这与近期历史明显不同，自 2023 年人工智能热潮爆发以来，标普 500 指数市值加权指数表现强劲：

信息很明确：投资者正在扩大赌注。 人工智能行业正在进入一个新阶段，涨势正在蔓延至传统领头羊之外。 如果历史有任何迹象的话，那么扩大就是一个看涨信号。 这意味着信心正在整个市场蔓延，它自然会对冲反弹，使反弹更加持久。

摩根士丹利首席信息官迈克·威尔逊（Mike Wilson）以典型的方式对此表示关注。 几周来，他一直在鼓吹市场扩大。 最近的一份报告详细说明了四个原因：

  1. 加速更多行业的盈利增长
  2. 扩大人工智能的采用
  3. 油价最终下跌，以及
  4. 美联储预计将继续按兵不动

威尔逊更进一步，将非必需消费品和运输业确定为他的两个首选行业，并指出这两个行业在过去两个月里已经跑赢了标准普尔 500 指数 12%。

更重要的是，威尔逊认为人工智能的扩展有超大规模卷土重来的空间。 他指出，超大规模企业已经通过承诺在未来几年投入巨额资本支出而撕掉了创可贴，而芯片制造商现在才感受到了压力。

高盛(Goldman Sachs)美国股票策略主管本·斯奈德(Ben Snider)最近向BI表达了同样的观点。 他的论点更注重估值，指出超大规模企业最近的交易价格接近其长期区间的底部，并且即将反弹。

对于愿意倾听的投资者来说，市场传达的信息并不是人工智能行业正在消退。 而是领导层正在以健康的方式发生变化。 最好的反弹并不依赖于一组股票来带动。 他们寻找新的领导者，吸引新的部门，并扩大他们的基础。 当前的看起来就是这样做的。

阅读下一篇

Joe Ciolli 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

乔是《商业内幕》的执行编辑，也是每日市场通讯《First Trade》的作者。 在此注册。他负责新闻编辑室的市场和投资报道，此前还负责管理经济和金融团队。 他于 2017 年 4 月开始在 Business Insider 担任记者。在加入 BI 之前，他是彭博社的股票记者，还在专注于外汇、债券和并购的团队工作过。 在加入 Bloomberg 之前，他曾在 CIBC World Markets 和 Navigant Capital Advisors 担任投资银行分析师。Joe 拥有斯坦福大学新闻学硕士学位和圣路易斯华盛顿大学工商管理学学士学位。
